title: quassia arnhemensis
intro: Quassia arnhemensis, also known as the Northern Quassia, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating skin conditions, due to its active compounds like quassin and limonoids. Quassia arnhemensis can be prepared into herbal remedies like tea, tincture, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
